title: Mastering English Phonics: V, W, Th, and Core Greetings
learningGoal: The learner will accurately map correct lip and tongue placement to physically pronounce challenging English sounds and execute smooth, confident greetings without heavy Indian accent markers.
instructions: In this extensive pronunciation trainer, you will practice the most difficult sounds for Hindi speakers. Listen to the native audio, pay attention to the phonetic lip placement hints, and record your voice trying to precisely mimic the native speaker. Aim for 80% accuracy to pass.

contentItems.text: Correct pronunciation of English requires moving your mouth slightly differently than Hindi. Focus on the V, W, and Th sounds during these heavy drills.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Place your top teeth on your bottom lip and push air out (vvvv-an).
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Pronouncing it like 'Wæn' with round lips

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Round your lips like a tight circle (ooh-wa-ter).
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Pronouncing it with teeth on lips like 'Vater'

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Round your lips completely before making the sound.
Put your tongue between your teeth and blow air out softly.
Tongue between teeth gently! Th-ank you.
Tongue behind teeth, vocal fold vibrating. Like the Hindi 'द' but softer.
Hold the 'R' in morning very lightly.
Blend the d and a together (Goo-dafternoon).
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Making a hard stop between good and afternoon

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Put emphasis on the second syllable: he-LOH.
Blend words, 'are' becomes very quick and soft.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Emphasizing all words equally like a robot

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Fine requires the 'F' sound—teeth on lips!

expectedPronunciationText: Van, Very, Water, Welcome, Think, Thank you, That, The, Good morning, Good afternoon, Good evening, Good night, Hello, How are you, I am fine, Goodbye

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! Your mouth movements are adapting wonderfully to the English phonetic system.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing in front of a mirror! Make sure your teeth touch your lips for V, and your lips become round for W.
feedbackRules.onPartial: You are getting closer! Focus specifically on maintaining a soft 'R' sound and rounding your lips for W.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: Van, Very, Water, Welcome, Think, Thank you, That, The, Good morning, Good afternoon, Good evening, Good night, Hello, How are you, I am fine, Goodbye
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: V requires teeth on lip. W requires round lips. Th requires tongue near teeth.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Van. Very. Water. Welcome. Think. Thank you. That. The. Good morning. Good afternoon. Good evening. Good night. Hello. How are you. I am fine. Goodbye.

hintText: Use a pocket mirror: if your bottom lip is completely hidden when you say 'Very', you are pronouncing it wrong!
commonMistakes: Merging V and W into a single 'wa' sound
